remarks.name: Dataiku Blog
remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: Enterprise AI transformation: how leading organizations are redesigning operations around AI
remarks.content: Most enterprises have run AI pilots. But few have transformed their operations with AI at scale. What separates them is organizational design, not technology: disconnected people, disconnected systems, disconnected governance. According to "Global AI confessions report: data lead
remarks.contentTranslation: 大多数企业都运行AI试点。但很少有人用AI大规模地改变了他们的运营。将它们区分开来的是组织设计，而不是技术：断开的人员、断开的系统、断开的治理。根据《全球AI认罪报告：数据线索
remarks.translation: 企业AI转型：领先组织如何围绕AI重新设计运营
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text: From partnership to pipeline: how Snowflake and Dataiku drove 200%+ YoY sourced opportunity growth
remarks.content: Partnerships are often judged too early, or on the wrong things. Visibility. Executive alignment. Event presence. A well-worded press release. None of that is the real test.
remarks.contentTranslation: 人们往往过早地判断伙伴关系，或者判断错误。可见性。高管一致性。事件存在。措辞良好的新闻稿。这些都不是真正的考验。
remarks.translation: 从合作伙伴关系到渠道： Snowflake和Dataiku如何推动200% +同比机会增长

remarks.role: AI governance, enterprise AI platform deployment, MLOps, analytics, agent orchestration.
remarks.text: How enterprise analytics platforms are evolving: from dashboards to AI-driven decisions
remarks.content: The enterprise analytics platforms of 2023 were dashboard factories. The ones built for 2026 are decision engines where AI agents, machine learning (ML) models, and business rules operate alongside traditional reporting. But many enterprises still cannot route model outputs into decision
remarks.contentTranslation: 2023年的企业分析平台是仪表板工厂。为2026年构建的决策引擎是AIAgent、机器学习(ML)模型和业务规则与传统报告一起运行的决策引擎。但许多企业仍然无法将模型输出转化为决策
remarks.translation: 企业分析平台如何发展：从仪表板到AI驱动的决策

remarks.name: Aditya Agarwal
remarks.role: General Partner SouthPkCommons, Co-Founder Bevel_Health
remarks.text:
I have been thinking about this photo a lot.

It's a beautiful testament to human civilization and our ability to reach for the stars.

But the thing that I can't get over is that a *human being* took this photo. From a tin cylinder flying on the other side of the moon. 

A machine could have taken it and it would have still been cool. But man was there. And that makes it so much more evocative.
remarks.translation: 我一直在想这张照片。这是对人类文明和我们到达星星的能力的美好证明。但我无法忘记的是，这张照片是一个*人类*拍摄的。来自在月球另一侧飞行的锡圆柱体。机器本可以拿走它，但它仍然很酷。但人就在那里。这使得它更令人回味无穷。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
The layer that can route to the best AI model for the particular job is going to increase in value substantially. There are at least 3 big reasons: 

* Cost optimization: there are plenty of use cases where you need frontier intelligence for some tasks and something far cheaper for others. Even in the same task you may use frontier intelligence for planning and review of the work, but an OSS or cheaper model for the bulk of the workload. This is going to be standard across large buckets of work going forward. 

* Capability maximization: despite the bitter lesson and models generally getting better in the same direction, there are still lots of differences between models. Some are better at tool use, others better at coding, and others again better at certain domains of knowledge work. The ability to route between these at different times is a huge advantage.

* Risk mitigation: while the Fable situation is somewhat of a black swan, it’s possible we’re heading toward a regulatory environment where governments may restrict models at different times based on their approval mechanisms or new things they discover. This means you’re going to want flexibility in being able to deploy workloads across different providers as a form of risk mitigation.

Ultimately, it’s going to increasingly be a a strategic advantage for the applied AI layer that they can effectively route between models. Will be very interesting to see how this evolves.
remarks.translation:
可以为特定作业路由到最佳AI模型的层的价值将大幅增加。至少有3个主要原因： *成本优化：有很多用例中，某些任务需要前沿智能，而其他任务则需要更便宜的用例。即使在同一任务中，您也可以使用前沿情报来规划和审查工作，但对于大部分工作量，您可以使用OSS或更便宜的模型。
在未来的大量工作中，这将是标准的。*能力最大化：尽管痛苦的教训和模型通常在同一方向上变得更好，但模型之间仍然存在很多差异。有些人更擅长使用工具，有些人更擅长编码，还有一些人更擅长知识工作的某些领域。在不同时间在这些之间进行路由的能力是一个巨大的优势。
*风险缓解：虽然寓言的情况有点像黑天鹅，但我们可能正在走向一个监管环境，政府可能会根据批准机制或发现的新事物在不同时间限制模型。这意味着您将需要灵活性，以便能够在不同的提供商之间部署工作负载，作为一种风险缓解形式。
最终，它将越来越成为应用AI层的战略优势，他们可以在模型之间有效地路由。看看这是如何演变的，会非常有趣。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Madhu Guru
remarks.role: Prev: Product Leader at Google - Gemini, Veo, Nano Banana.
remarks.text:
Having been through many frontier model launch reviews, I have empathy for everyone involved.

Launching an LLM isn't like shipping traditional software - you're making a decision about a black box with effectively infinite use cases and infinite failure modes.

The tradeoffs are hard - every increase in capability expands the space of both valuable use cases and potential misuse.

As a lab, you build extensive evals, you red-team, you iterate on the model. You debate tradeoffs across candidate checkpoints before choosing the best one to launch.

Then early-access partners still uncover behaviors you didn't anticipate.

You can never be 100% certain you've understood a frontier model. You focus on reducing the uncertainty enough to launch.

As frontier models become smarter across the industry, that decision will get harder - for labs and regulators.
remarks.translation:
我经历了许多前沿模式的发布评论，我对参与的每个人都有同情心。推出LLM不像运送传统软件-您正在决定一个具有有效无限用例和无限故障模式的黑匣子。权衡是困难的--能力的每一次提高都会扩大有价值的用例和潜在的滥用空间。作为一个实验室，你建立了广泛的evals ，你红队，你迭代模型。
在选择最佳启动检查点之前，您需要在候选人检查点之间进行权衡。然后，抢先体验的合作伙伴仍然会发现您没有预料到的行为。你永远无法百分之百确定你已经理解了一个前沿模型。您专注于减少不确定性，足以启动。随着前沿模型在整个行业变得更加智能，对于实验室和监管机构来说，这一决定将变得更加困难。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 AI 编程工具、设计到代码流程和软件生产方式的变化；需要结合产品发布或文档再做事实判断。

remarks.name: Swyx
remarks.role: achieve ambition with intentionality, intensity, integrity & insanity.
remarks.text:
Last chance to fill out the annual AI Engineering Survey this weekend and win great Vercel + Notion + AIE tix!

link below 

we had @devinai analyze registered attendee list and output a live chart of the people coming to the conference. it ended up being the single best data driven storytelling i've ever seen on what kind of community we are gathering in two weeks.

survey link here!
https://t.co/2oilc5lCDm

no lurking, fill it out pls
remarks.translation: 本周末填写年度AI工程调查的最后机会，赢得伟大的Vercel + Notion + AIE tix ！下面的链接，我们有@ devinai分析注册与会者名单，并输出参加会议的人员的实时图表。它最终成为我见过的关于我们在两周内聚集的社区类型的最佳数据驱动的故事讲述。调查链接这里！没有潜伏，请填写

remarks.role: partner fpvventures - investing in seed/A. previous: early hire meter, opendoor, atlassian & others. love shimoleejhaveri + 👦👧
remarks.text:
Not sure which founder needs to hear this..

but paid partnerships and boosting “views” on X are now negative signal in all VC group chats.

I’m hoping this comes to the inflating ARR trend soon, but that might still take another cycle.
remarks.translation: 不确定哪位创始人需要听到这个消息……但付费合作伙伴关系和提升对X的“观点”现在在所有风险投资群组聊天中都是负面的信号。我希望这很快就会出现ARR上涨的趋势，但这可能需要另一个周期。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察 builder 对产品、创业、销售或市场节奏的判断；可作为选题线索，不直接等同于行业结论。

remarks.text:
In AI most people are still trying to use old maps on a new territory. 

Throw the maps away. It's time to draw new ones. The only way you can do it is walking the land.

remarks.name: Aaron Levie
remarks.role: ceo box - your business lives in content. unleash it with AI
remarks.text:
This whole Fable export control situation is actually net positive to regulation discourse. It’s an early peek into what AI regulation would end up looking like at scale when enacted at the model layer instead of the specific application of the AI.

The government would have sole discretion over when a model can be released to the to public, based on a bunch of factors that they inherently control. 

In this case, based on the available reporting, the risk is that the model can be jailbroken to deliver increased cyber exploit capabilities. The issue is that actually you want models to be able to have those capabilities on the defense side of cyber as well, and for all intents and purposes, by Anthropic’s own response, you can execute these capabilities today in other models. 

So thus the whole challenge will be that you’re debating with the government, over months and months, with every model release, what these models are actually capable of and what their risks are. Inherently, there’s not only a lot of subjectivity in determining those risks, but there’s also many other factors that go into the risks being practical in the first place.

The net result is that we would end up with backlog of AI releases, progress in the market inherently would dramatically slow down, and AI would start to look more like any other sclerotic industry. If this paradigm had existed 3 years ago at the start of the current AI wave, we’d likely currently be stuck on GPT-4 level intelligence at this point.

This is why, wherever possible, we should be regulating the applied use of AI. We should continue to study and enforce the dangerous use of AI in cyber attacks, financial services risks, fraud, biowarfare, and other spaces. AI safety is incredibly important, but slowing down progress this early in the development of AI I suspect is net harmful.
remarks.translation:
整个寓言出口管制情况实际上对监管话语是积极的。这是对AI监管在模型层而不是AI的具体应用中最终看起来像什么的早期窥视。政府将根据其固有控制的一系列因素，自行决定何时向公众发布模型。
在这种情况下，根据可用的报告，风险在于可以越狱模型以提供更高的网络利用能力。问题在于，实际上您希望模型也能够在网络防御方面拥有这些功能，并且无论出于何种意图和目的，通过Anthropic自己的反应，您今天可以在其他模型中执行这些功能。
因此，整个挑战将是您在每个模型发布时与政府进行数月和数月的辩论，这些模型实际上能够做什么以及它们的风险是什么。从本质上讲，在确定这些风险时不仅有很多主观性，而且还有许多其他因素会影响风险的实际性。
最终的结果是，我们最终会积压大量的AI发布，市场的进展本质上会急剧放缓，AI将开始看起来更像任何其他僵化行业。如果在当前的AI浪潮开始时，这种范式在3年前就已经存在，那么我们目前可能还停留在GPT-4级别的智能上。这就是为什么我们应该尽可能规范AI的应用使用。
我们应该继续研究和实施AI在网络攻击、金融服务风险、欺诈、生物战等领域的危险使用。AI安全非常重要，但我怀疑，在AI发展的早期阶段，放慢进展速度是绝对有害的。
remarks.observation: 这条言论适合观察模型运行、算力、存储、sandbox 或开发平台基础设施的变化；后续事实判断需回到原始公告或技术文档。
